# Broad Mites

by Nebula Haze
**Broad mites** (_Polyphagotarsonemus latus_) on your cannabis plants are so tiny they are practically impossible to spot with the naked eye. They are about 1/3 the size of a spider mite, and therefore broad mites are even difficult to see under a microscope. Most growers notice the symptoms of broad mites on their cannabis plants long before they ever see a broad mite or broad mite eggs.
With broad mites, the new growth may be twisted, blistered and “wet” looking. If your marijuana plant is flowering, the buds may turn brown and die.
These cannabis broad mite damage pics were taken by _Hosttrevor_(thank you!)
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/08/example-broad-mite-damage-cannabis-glossy-blistered-leaves.jpg)
Dark-colored broad mite under 100x magnification by [David B. Langston](https://www.forestryimages.org/browse/detail.cfm?imgnum=5077061) (University of Georgia)
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2017/10/broad-mite-Polyphagotarsonemus-latus-David-B-Langston-university-of-georgia.jpg)
## Broad Mite Symptoms:
  * Bugs are so small they are difficult to see without magnification
  * Under a microscope, you can see that they have 6 legs when young (as larvae), and 8 legs as adults
  * Symptoms are often confused for [heat stress](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems/heat-stress-cannabis), [overwatering](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems/over-watering-cannabis), a [pH imbalance](https://www.growweedeasy.com/ph), or [root problems](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems/root-problems-cannabis)
  * New growth may be twisted or drooping
  * Leaves may be, blistered or “wet” looking
  * Leaves may be turned up at the edges
  * Broad mites don’t attack all parts of the plant evenly; symptoms are worse in certain spots where the infestation is concentrated
  * In the flowering stage, buds may become sickly and die

One of the main symptoms of broad mites on cannabis is that the newest growth is coming in twisted.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2017/02/twisted-new-growth-from-cannabis-broad-mites.jpg)
Edges may turn up as if the plant is suffering from heat stress, but with broad the leaves take on a glossy, almost plastic-like appearance. Eventually affected leaves turn yellow or bronze and die.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/08/example-broad-mite-damage-cannabis-glossy-blistered-leaves.jpg)
The main way to spot an infestation is the damage they leave behind because usually the mites themselves are too small to see. They like to hang out inside inside your plants, where they can live and lay eggs without you seeing them.
Some broad mites appear white or yellow colored. Picture by [Bruce Watt](https://www.forestryimages.org/browse/detail.cfm?imgnum=5530095) from the University of Maine.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2017/10/broad-mite-Polyphagotarsonemus-latus-Banks-by-Bruce-Watt-university-of-maine.jpg)
Sometimes the symptoms can be confused for [tobacco mosaic virus](https://www.growweedeasy.com/tobacco-mosaic-virus-tmv-cannabis). Leaf symptoms from broad mites are also commonly misdiagnosed as [overwatering](https://www.growweedeasy.com/overwatering), a [nutrient deficiency](https://www.growweedeasy.com/cannabis-symptoms-pictures) or [heat damage](https://www.growweedeasy.com/heat-light-stress).
One of the biggest reasons broad mites can be so difficult to diagnose is you rarely see any signs of bugs, and you don’t see bites on the leaves. For many people, they don’t even realize a bug infestation is happening.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/08/cannabis-broad-mite-damage-leaves-discovered-shiny-edges-up.jpg)[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/08/marijuana-broad-mite-damage-blistered-leaves-twisted-growth.jpg)
Sometimes top leaves droop. In this pic you can see that part of the droopy leaves are getting that blistered, wet appearance from broad mites.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2017/02/example-of-drooping-top-leaves-caused-by-broad-mite-damage-marijuana.jpg)
The following marijuana plant has been treated for broad mites and is starting to recover. You can see the newest growth looks matte and healthy, while the damaged leaves from before still look glossy and blistered.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/08/example-broad-mite-damage-marijuana-plant-is-starting-to-recover.jpg)
It’s great if your plant is starting to look healthy again, but even if your plant appears to be recovering, don’t stop on weekly treatments for at least a few weeks. The broad mites could still be there waiting to take over the plant again! They are the masters of hiding and waiting.
Unfortunately, broad mites are probably one of the hardest marijuana pests to get rid of. They lay their eggs  _inside_ the plant which makes it very difficult to kill their eggs!
Broad mite under 100x magnification, by [Chazz Hesselein](https://www.forestryimages.org/browse/detail.cfm?imgnum=5438919&)
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2017/10/broad-mite-100x-magnification-Chazz-Hesselein.jpg)
## Solution: How to Get Rid of Broad Mites On Cannabis
Once you’ve actually identified that you have broad mites, it’s time to get rid of them! When it comes to broad mites, a lot of the “standard” miticides are not as effective and you’ll notice these particular mites aren’t listed on the labels.
Broad mites can be one of the toughest marijuana pests to get rid of, but it can be done if you stay vigilant.
  * **Immediately and carefully remove infected parts of the plant** – if there’s already an infestation in the tissue, you likely won’t be able to save those particular leaves/buds. Your main goal is to stop the infestation from spreading.
  * **Treat often** – You may have to treat several times a week or even daily if you have a terrible infestation that won’t go away. This can be very difficult on your plants.
  * **Spray Before Lights Go Out** – Whenever treating plants with a spray, do it before the lights go off so that your plant is less likely to get burned.
  * **Read Instructions** – Make sure to _read_ the full instructions of each bottle and follow them when treating your plants. You will save yourself a lot of big headaches.
  * **Repeat treatments weekly for 5 more weeks after mites are gone** – After you think broad mites are completely gone, don’t stop. Treat your plant with a different treatment at least once a week for 5 additional weeks. If you don’t completely eradicate them they’ll come back with a vengeance and can be even more resistant to whatever you throw at them.

Here’s a Detailed Step-by-Step:
**1.) Dispose of known infected plant matter**
The parts of the plant that are already infested should be carefully removed and discarded of if possible. This will dramatically reduce the bug numbers, and help save the rest of your plant.
**2.) Neem Oil**
[Neem Oil](https://www.amazon.com/Organic-Neem-Bliss-100-Pressed/dp/B0716JF8MB?crid=27KCMJJ7SJ728&dib=eyJ2IjoiMSJ9.zJceftaoFsbY-SgBmFMQG-6L6HwJTLfDObGn_uUnaVufeoF45V2prdIrgkr_dK7HBFw9RPua7TCqjCuMRJF2_ncb-OfXTEPnvAr4kYJlaJYlHZ2I0MV4hPVDu3VwD-Mxwb5Lk2AlU5TfmXVNJn9grK4O9ofsbWUk5EZ0sVYU2BzxrEep5zUQk1GUb1SWdgPqCJhTZHhtj2s6ll9USiWWXvVKgh-0fGpfBl6GzZLJDdfmTT7qYjRlFk6a5bNP7iLIG70HgDVa-C43YutAd_2PBNYWfKRNFO9xcWQQUrQ9xCo.LaE3BNIzXEMKNneZ1RAQ5Ry8cnxkG_X4boBFKYfTCtI&dib_tag=se&keywords=neem%2Boil&qid=1726430381&sprefix=neem%2Boil%2Caps%2C300&sr=8-1-spons&sp_csd=d2lkZ2V0TmFtZT1zcF9hdGY&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=fbf2a755b86cfbda5bfa17837da5d8b3&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) will leave an unpleasant taste/smell on buds when used to treat flowering plants, so don’t let this stuff get near your buds. There’s also some evidence Neem oil may be [harmful to humans](http://www.ajnr.org/content/31/7/E60.full) so use it with care. That being said, Neem oil is an all-natural remedy (it’s actually the most popular natural pesticide in the _world_) that is very effective against many different types of bugs and mold, including broad mites.
Neem oil can be rough on your plants so you don’t want to use it more often than you have to because your plants may suffer. However, if you get desperate treating plants _daily_ with Neem oil can be effective at killing broad mites when nothing seems to be working.
You will need a [mister](https://www.amazon.com/Chapin-1002-48-Ounce-Sprayer-Multi-purpose/dp/B00002N8OB/ref=as_li_ss_il?_encoding=UTF8&pd_rd_i=B00002N8OB&pd_rd_r=W4A5MVZWMFWH74AT55F1&pd_rd_w=UPVXy&pd_rd_wg=EwzKa&psc=1&refRID=W4A5MVZWMFWH74AT55F1&linkCode=li2&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=be39a1c7300f65ee56613c1b764f0227) (also called a “One-Hand Pressure Sprayer”) to spray all the leaves evenly since neem oil and water can separate easily. A mister helps you get full and even coverage on all parts of the plant.
[](https://www.amazon.com/Organic-Neem-Bliss-100-Pressed/dp/B0716JF8MB?crid=27KCMJJ7SJ728&dib=eyJ2IjoiMSJ9.zJceftaoFsbY-SgBmFMQG-6L6HwJTLfDObGn_uUnaVufeoF45V2prdIrgkr_dK7HBFw9RPua7TCqjCuMRJF2_ncb-OfXTEPnvAr4kYJlaJYlHZ2I0MV4hPVDu3VwD-Mxwb5Lk2AlU5TfmXVNJn9grK4O9ofsbWUk5EZ0sVYU2BzxrEep5zUQk1GUb1SWdgPqCJhTZHhtj2s6ll9USiWWXvVKgh-0fGpfBl6GzZLJDdfmTT7qYjRlFk6a5bNP7iLIG70HgDVa-C43YutAd_2PBNYWfKRNFO9xcWQQUrQ9xCo.LaE3BNIzXEMKNneZ1RAQ5Ry8cnxkG_X4boBFKYfTCtI&dib_tag=se&keywords=neem%2Boil&qid=1726430381&sprefix=neem%2Boil%2Caps%2C300&sr=8-1-spons&sp_csd=d2lkZ2V0TmFtZT1zcF9hdGY&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=fbf2a755b86cfbda5bfa17837da5d8b3&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) [](https://www.amazon.com/Chapin-International-10031-Multi-Purpose-Translucent/dp/B08Q5SWVFC?crid=189UZDGV39P77&dib=eyJ2IjoiMSJ9.HAE1qrf46IjvCDTKg8qAhfEeukBH6P2yCaSCmrdZIl84LxHf8aunz2vOQY3qacwMD2vPyBm_62mDoJIv2RLuLFO5DR15kvDH6K0xecq-AzbE8Klhod16FQGD6HkB48RcfI3CP4zgcXNiOqX92unWL6fD8Vq6mFPzOJOGjUFTlmsJXRL5U0npyqA1EBElS_dFJ47B49Zlen1xC2qmc7kD7UZ7m2axsLwCMTcVoCkCpEOUTft_kCJbPmlTwkuAjrCY3iFmBwi08Ot9vvP35drzltkHS1AEmfuGMza7ONc9NMQ.bohCskGf0py55saKnrn7B_hY8Dvt8710Hgi0z4BNvRo&dib_tag=se&keywords=garden%2Bpressure%2Bsprayer&qid=1726433516&s=lawn-garden&sprefix=garden%2Bpressure%2Bsprayer%2Clawngarden%2C136&sr=1-8&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=af4d34fb510ec81cf6baa702ba75a7db&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)
**3.) Mite-B-Gone**
Insecticidal soap-like products that contain fatty acid salts or sulfate esters weaken the outer shell of mites but are safe for humans and don’t leave much of a residue. Sodium Lauryl Sulfate (SLS) is a sulfate ester that’s particularly good against mites.
With soaps and similar products, coverage is very important as it does not stay on your plant for long, so follow-up applications may be necessary. Although this is considered safe, avoid getting any on your buds so it doesn’t affect the taste/smell!
[Recommended: Mite Killer Spray by Mite-B-Gone](https://www.amazon.com/Mite-Killer-Spray-Mite-B-Gone-Treatment/dp/B0CKM16ZPL?th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=c21aa72c3444c0e198ddc0403e158560&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) – In addition to Sodium Lauryl Sulfate, this one also contains Citric Acid, and Sodium Chloride. These are all non-toxic and friendly to the environment, while being a menace to broad mites on your cannabis plants.
[Mite Killer Spray by Mite-B-Gone](https://www.amazon.com/Mite-Killer-Spray-Mite-B-Gone-Treatment/dp/B0CKM16ZPL?th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=c21aa72c3444c0e198ddc0403e158560&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)
[](https://www.amazon.com/Mite-Killer-Spray-Mite-B-Gone-Treatment/dp/B0CKM16ZPL?th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=c21aa72c3444c0e198ddc0403e158560&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)
**4.) Standard insecticidal soap**
Fatty acid salts or [insecticidal soaps](https://www.amazon.com/Bayer-Advanced-NATRIA-Insecticidal-24-Ounce/dp/B00364PEFS?s=home-garden&ie=UTF8&qid=1525731097&sr=1-1&keywords=insecticidal%2Bsoap&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=2eeaf00590755a5d1dfe352f0e4a589f&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) can help against broad mites. They weaken the outer shell of broad mites but are safe to use on your plants and they don’t leave much of a residue which could kill beneficial bugs in your garden.
Follow the directions and avoid getting any on your buds because it can affect the taste or smell.
This probably won’t get rid of broad mites on its own, but it is less harsh on your plants than some of the other options and so it can be a great way to supplement the other treatments you’re doing by bringing down their numbers.
[Natria Insecticidal Soap Insect Killer](https://www.amazon.com/Bayer-Advanced-NATRIA-Insecticidal-24-Ounce/dp/B00364PEFS?s=home-garden&ie=UTF8&qid=1525731097&sr=1-1&keywords=insecticidal%2Bsoap&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=2eeaf00590755a5d1dfe352f0e4a589f&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)
[](https://www.amazon.com/Bayer-Advanced-NATRIA-Insecticidal-24-Ounce/dp/B00364PEFS?s=home-garden&ie=UTF8&qid=1525731097&sr=1-1&keywords=insecticidal%2Bsoap&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=2eeaf00590755a5d1dfe352f0e4a589f&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)
**5.) Earth’s Ally 3-in-1 Plant Spray (organic)**
[Earth’s Ally 3-in-1 Plant Spray](https://www.amazon.com/Earths-Ally-Concentrate-Insecticide-Houseplants/dp/B09LYXWWQN?crid=16WC0FVVV7ERR&dib=eyJ2IjoiMSJ9.Y3HiD_VRvAh2dEyNfjk65EDbzK8lCVjLZcJkmiw6ZZrZ1a_Yr27v7hQQKg86vVrVb28q3PdQy2latVm8Y2VRs19kZnfYMHFo_LFMOrOy3YT6uSJ-2xnHHU_kwXoDG2ZY-EhyWGLaz0Bx4Yd-rq6_v5pxAw39NL2Je8EJXdn--7KeaOReY98hvY6iYiSzYhv7F6fIg9V96D_P4iP9piVqsAkgP28DpIHYXQzhQZzl8J4ZI9crGtlLBq-KfznMfQnXswk0TheQJ1muDsgxFxKez10a2aktD_YHmqxPVFJl9w4.wV9LrNtbIZfnoA9QuVXa6xiU-Oyy0EIMn08pMaoyMZM&dib_tag=se&keywords=spidermite%2Bkiller&qid=1726431689&s=lawn-garden&sprefix=spidermite%2Bkiller%2Clawngarden%2C121&sr=1-5&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=bc9fd53014dbe394b632ce5ed90cb1aa&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) is a mix of various horticultural oils which are organic and safe for humans. It is effective against many soft-bodied bugs, including broad mites to some extent, when the cannabis plants are treated regularly. Unfortunately, it only stays effective on the plant for about 24 hours so you will want to either apply this daily or (better idea) combine with other options and rotate between them. You will need a [mister](https://www.amazon.com/Chapin-International-10031-Multi-Purpose-Translucent/dp/B08Q5SWVFC?crid=189UZDGV39P77&dib=eyJ2IjoiMSJ9.HAE1qrf46IjvCDTKg8qAhfEeukBH6P2yCaSCmrdZIl84LxHf8aunz2vOQY3qacwMD2vPyBm_62mDoJIv2RLuLFO5DR15kvDH6K0xecq-AzbE8Klhod16FQGD6HkB48RcfI3CP4zgcXNiOqX92unWL6fD8Vq6mFPzOJOGjUFTlmsJXRL5U0npyqA1EBElS_dFJ47B49Zlen1xC2qmc7kD7UZ7m2axsLwCMTcVoCkCpEOUTft_kCJbPmlTwkuAjrCY3iFmBwi08Ot9vvP35drzltkHS1AEmfuGMza7ONc9NMQ.bohCskGf0py55saKnrn7B_hY8Dvt8710Hgi0z4BNvRo&dib_tag=se&keywords=garden%2Bpressure%2Bsprayer&qid=1726433516&s=lawn-garden&sprefix=garden%2Bpressure%2Bsprayer%2Clawngarden%2C136&sr=1-8&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=af4d34fb510ec81cf6baa702ba75a7db&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) (also called a “One-Hand Pressure Sprayer”) to spray all the leaves evenly.
[Earth’s Ally 3-in-1 Plant Spray](https://www.amazon.com/Earths-Ally-Concentrate-Insecticide-Houseplants/dp/B09LYXWWQN?crid=16WC0FVVV7ERR&dib=eyJ2IjoiMSJ9.Y3HiD_VRvAh2dEyNfjk65EDbzK8lCVjLZcJkmiw6ZZrZ1a_Yr27v7hQQKg86vVrVb28q3PdQy2latVm8Y2VRs19kZnfYMHFo_LFMOrOy3YT6uSJ-2xnHHU_kwXoDG2ZY-EhyWGLaz0Bx4Yd-rq6_v5pxAw39NL2Je8EJXdn--7KeaOReY98hvY6iYiSzYhv7F6fIg9V96D_P4iP9piVqsAkgP28DpIHYXQzhQZzl8J4ZI9crGtlLBq-KfznMfQnXswk0TheQJ1muDsgxFxKez10a2aktD_YHmqxPVFJl9w4.wV9LrNtbIZfnoA9QuVXa6xiU-Oyy0EIMn08pMaoyMZM&dib_tag=se&keywords=spidermite%2Bkiller&qid=1726431689&s=lawn-garden&sprefix=spidermite%2Bkiller%2Clawngarden%2C121&sr=1-5&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=bc9fd53014dbe394b632ce5ed90cb1aa&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) + [Mister](https://www.amazon.com/Chapin-International-10031-Multi-Purpose-Translucent/dp/B08Q5SWVFC?crid=189UZDGV39P77&dib=eyJ2IjoiMSJ9.HAE1qrf46IjvCDTKg8qAhfEeukBH6P2yCaSCmrdZIl84LxHf8aunz2vOQY3qacwMD2vPyBm_62mDoJIv2RLuLFO5DR15kvDH6K0xecq-AzbE8Klhod16FQGD6HkB48RcfI3CP4zgcXNiOqX92unWL6fD8Vq6mFPzOJOGjUFTlmsJXRL5U0npyqA1EBElS_dFJ47B49Zlen1xC2qmc7kD7UZ7m2axsLwCMTcVoCkCpEOUTft_kCJbPmlTwkuAjrCY3iFmBwi08Ot9vvP35drzltkHS1AEmfuGMza7ONc9NMQ.bohCskGf0py55saKnrn7B_hY8Dvt8710Hgi0z4BNvRo&dib_tag=se&keywords=garden%2Bpressure%2Bsprayer&qid=1726433516&s=lawn-garden&sprefix=garden%2Bpressure%2Bsprayer%2Clawngarden%2C136&sr=1-8&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=af4d34fb510ec81cf6baa702ba75a7db&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)
[](https://www.amazon.com/Earths-Ally-Concentrate-Insecticide-Houseplants/dp/B09LYXWWQN?crid=16WC0FVVV7ERR&dib=eyJ2IjoiMSJ9.Y3HiD_VRvAh2dEyNfjk65EDbzK8lCVjLZcJkmiw6ZZrZ1a_Yr27v7hQQKg86vVrVb28q3PdQy2latVm8Y2VRs19kZnfYMHFo_LFMOrOy3YT6uSJ-2xnHHU_kwXoDG2ZY-EhyWGLaz0Bx4Yd-rq6_v5pxAw39NL2Je8EJXdn--7KeaOReY98hvY6iYiSzYhv7F6fIg9V96D_P4iP9piVqsAkgP28DpIHYXQzhQZzl8J4ZI9crGtlLBq-KfznMfQnXswk0TheQJ1muDsgxFxKez10a2aktD_YHmqxPVFJl9w4.wV9LrNtbIZfnoA9QuVXa6xiU-Oyy0EIMn08pMaoyMZM&dib_tag=se&keywords=spidermite%2Bkiller&qid=1726431689&s=lawn-garden&sprefix=spidermite%2Bkiller%2Clawngarden%2C121&sr=1-5&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=49ca708b5e58518e69723b857f280d69&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) [](https://www.amazon.com/Chapin-International-10031-Multi-Purpose-Translucent/dp/B08Q5SWVFC?crid=189UZDGV39P77&dib=eyJ2IjoiMSJ9.HAE1qrf46IjvCDTKg8qAhfEeukBH6P2yCaSCmrdZIl84LxHf8aunz2vOQY3qacwMD2vPyBm_62mDoJIv2RLuLFO5DR15kvDH6K0xecq-AzbE8Klhod16FQGD6HkB48RcfI3CP4zgcXNiOqX92unWL6fD8Vq6mFPzOJOGjUFTlmsJXRL5U0npyqA1EBElS_dFJ47B49Zlen1xC2qmc7kD7UZ7m2axsLwCMTcVoCkCpEOUTft_kCJbPmlTwkuAjrCY3iFmBwi08Ot9vvP35drzltkHS1AEmfuGMza7ONc9NMQ.bohCskGf0py55saKnrn7B_hY8Dvt8710Hgi0z4BNvRo&dib_tag=se&keywords=garden%2Bpressure%2Bsprayer&qid=1726433516&s=lawn-garden&sprefix=garden%2Bpressure%2Bsprayer%2Clawngarden%2C136&sr=1-8&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=af4d34fb510ec81cf6baa702ba75a7db&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)
**6.)****Diatomaceous Earth**
[Diatomaceous Earth](https://www.amazon.com/HARRIS-Diatomaceous-Crawling-Insect-Included/dp/B082LRSCV8?dchild=1&keywords=Diatomaceous+Earth&qid=1628982195&sr=8-1-spons&psc=1&spLa=ZW5jcnlwdGVkUXVhbGlmaWVyPUExU1IwN0VLT0kwRVFWJmVuY3J5cHRlZElkPUEwMTQ5ODg1MTYxMkdOMkJLVkJIRyZlbmNyeXB0ZWRBZElkPUEwOTI2NDU0MU9LUU4xODcwTzZJUyZ3aWRnZXROYW1lPXNwX2F0ZiZhY3Rpb249Y2xpY2tSZWRpcmVjdCZkb05vdExvZ0NsaWNrPXRydWU%3D&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=19a474a2e8903b9e70c3852e43294f5e&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) is basically fossil dust that can be sprinkled on the top of your soil, and anywhere else in your room (window sills, doorways, etc). This powder-like substance is harmless to mammals and plants but is incredibly sharp at the microscopic level. Therefore it will tear and dehydrate broad mites on physical contact. This will **not** get rid of an infestation but can help prevent, control, and slow things down when used effectively. When it comes to broad mites, you want to use every tool you can.
[](https://www.amazon.com/HARRIS-Diatomaceous-Crawling-Insect-Included/dp/B082LRSCV8?dchild=1&keywords=Diatomaceous+Earth&qid=1628982195&sr=8-1-spons&psc=1&spLa=ZW5jcnlwdGVkUXVhbGlmaWVyPUExU1IwN0VLT0kwRVFWJmVuY3J5cHRlZElkPUEwMTQ5ODg1MTYxMkdOMkJLVkJIRyZlbmNyeXB0ZWRBZElkPUEwOTI2NDU0MU9LUU4xODcwTzZJUyZ3aWRnZXROYW1lPXNwX2F0ZiZhY3Rpb249Y2xpY2tSZWRpcmVjdCZkb05vdExvZ0NsaWNrPXRydWU%3D&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=6e0a96683124d0e17ca2c8a533ad3e4f&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)
**7.) Heat**
Broad mites don’t like the heat above 90°F (32°C). Some growers will try to get rid of them by on small plants or clones by dunking the plants in hot water (105°F / 40°C) for 10-20 minutes. I’ve also heard of growers try to reduce their number by overheating the grow space to 115°F (46°C) for an hour. Be careful as this can be dangerous if you don’t take safety precautions, and any heat method strong enough to kill the mites will likely hurt your plants.
**8.) Predatory Mites**
Some types of predatory mites, like  _Neoseiulus_ type mites, love to eat broad mites. Supplementing your garden with extra predators can help bring down broad mite numbers. However, it’s not enough to fix the problem on their own and unfortunately, a lot of the other remedies on this page will also kill predatory mites.
If you don’t want to use pesticides, or want some extra help, get [predatory mites](https://www.amazon.com/000-Live-Adult-Predatory-Mites/dp/B00K60CEOO?ie=UTF8&qid=1470528454&sr=8-1&keywords=Neoseiulus&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=c6507ad8232bf3fdae608d010ed992f1&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) to help eat all your broad mites.
[](https://www.amazon.com/000-Live-Adult-Predatory-Mites/dp/B00K60CEOO?ie=UTF8&qid=1470528454&sr=8-1&keywords=Neoseiulus&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=c6507ad8232bf3fdae608d010ed992f1&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)
**9.) AgroMagen GrowSafe (Organic)**
GrowSafe by AgroMagen is a natural miticide that claims to be effective against broad mites. I am not very familiar with this product, but have heard good things about it. Given how tough this pest can be to get rid of, I wanted to make sure I included every possible option. From what I understand you will need to spray more than once and this is best combined with at least one other treatment. You will need a [mister](https://www.amazon.com/Chapin-International-10031-Multi-Purpose-Translucent/dp/B08Q5SWVFC?crid=189UZDGV39P77&dib=eyJ2IjoiMSJ9.HAE1qrf46IjvCDTKg8qAhfEeukBH6P2yCaSCmrdZIl84LxHf8aunz2vOQY3qacwMD2vPyBm_62mDoJIv2RLuLFO5DR15kvDH6K0xecq-AzbE8Klhod16FQGD6HkB48RcfI3CP4zgcXNiOqX92unWL6fD8Vq6mFPzOJOGjUFTlmsJXRL5U0npyqA1EBElS_dFJ47B49Zlen1xC2qmc7kD7UZ7m2axsLwCMTcVoCkCpEOUTft_kCJbPmlTwkuAjrCY3iFmBwi08Ot9vvP35drzltkHS1AEmfuGMza7ONc9NMQ.bohCskGf0py55saKnrn7B_hY8Dvt8710Hgi0z4BNvRo&dib_tag=se&keywords=garden%2Bpressure%2Bsprayer&qid=1726433516&s=lawn-garden&sprefix=garden%2Bpressure%2Bsprayer%2Clawngarden%2C136&sr=1-8&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=af4d34fb510ec81cf6baa702ba75a7db&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) (also called a “One-Hand Pressure Sprayer”) to spray all the leaves evenly.
[GrowSafe](https://www.amazon.com/Bio-Pesticide-All-Natural-Insecticide-Mildew-Non-Phytotoxic-Concentrate/dp/B07M983TRL?dchild=1&keywords=broad%2Bmites&qid=1628982702&s=lawn-garden&sr=1-1-spons&spLa=ZW5jcnlwdGVkUXVhbGlmaWVyPUEzVFg5RzAwMEhMV1pHJmVuY3J5cHRlZElkPUEwMDEzODA5M0xKUzFaOEFSRjROOSZlbmNyeXB0ZWRBZElkPUEwMjEwNTQxMjlFRVNaSEwyNUtWRCZ3aWRnZXROYW1lPXNwX2F0ZiZhY3Rpb249Y2xpY2tSZWRpcmVjdCZkb05vdExvZ0NsaWNrPXRydWU%3D&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=03dcd02047764ea3428114123d6f0595&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) (plus a [mister](https://www.amazon.com/Chapin-International-10031-Multi-Purpose-Translucent/dp/B08Q5SWVFC?crid=189UZDGV39P77&dib=eyJ2IjoiMSJ9.HAE1qrf46IjvCDTKg8qAhfEeukBH6P2yCaSCmrdZIl84LxHf8aunz2vOQY3qacwMD2vPyBm_62mDoJIv2RLuLFO5DR15kvDH6K0xecq-AzbE8Klhod16FQGD6HkB48RcfI3CP4zgcXNiOqX92unWL6fD8Vq6mFPzOJOGjUFTlmsJXRL5U0npyqA1EBElS_dFJ47B49Zlen1xC2qmc7kD7UZ7m2axsLwCMTcVoCkCpEOUTft_kCJbPmlTwkuAjrCY3iFmBwi08Ot9vvP35drzltkHS1AEmfuGMza7ONc9NMQ.bohCskGf0py55saKnrn7B_hY8Dvt8710Hgi0z4BNvRo&dib_tag=se&keywords=garden%2Bpressure%2Bsprayer&qid=1726433516&s=lawn-garden&sprefix=garden%2Bpressure%2Bsprayer%2Clawngarden%2C136&sr=1-8&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=af4d34fb510ec81cf6baa702ba75a7db&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)) kills broad mites and almost any mite (including hemp russet mites and spider mites)
[](https://www.amazon.com/Bio-Pesticide-All-Natural-Insecticide-Mildew-Non-Phytotoxic-Concentrate/dp/B07M983TRL?dchild=1&keywords=broad%2Bmites&qid=1628982702&s=lawn-garden&sr=1-1-spons&spLa=ZW5jcnlwdGVkUXVhbGlmaWVyPUEzVFg5RzAwMEhMV1pHJmVuY3J5cHRlZElkPUEwMDEzODA5M0xKUzFaOEFSRjROOSZlbmNyeXB0ZWRBZElkPUEwMjEwNTQxMjlFRVNaSEwyNUtWRCZ3aWRnZXROYW1lPXNwX2F0ZiZhY3Rpb249Y2xpY2tSZWRpcmVjdCZkb05vdExvZ0NsaWNrPXRydWU%3D&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=03dcd02047764ea3428114123d6f0595&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) [](https://www.amazon.com/Chapin-International-10031-Multi-Purpose-Translucent/dp/B08Q5SWVFC?crid=189UZDGV39P77&dib=eyJ2IjoiMSJ9.HAE1qrf46IjvCDTKg8qAhfEeukBH6P2yCaSCmrdZIl84LxHf8aunz2vOQY3qacwMD2vPyBm_62mDoJIv2RLuLFO5DR15kvDH6K0xecq-AzbE8Klhod16FQGD6HkB48RcfI3CP4zgcXNiOqX92unWL6fD8Vq6mFPzOJOGjUFTlmsJXRL5U0npyqA1EBElS_dFJ47B49Zlen1xC2qmc7kD7UZ7m2axsLwCMTcVoCkCpEOUTft_kCJbPmlTwkuAjrCY3iFmBwi08Ot9vvP35drzltkHS1AEmfuGMza7ONc9NMQ.bohCskGf0py55saKnrn7B_hY8Dvt8710Hgi0z4BNvRo&dib_tag=se&keywords=garden%2Bpressure%2Bsprayer&qid=1726433516&s=lawn-garden&sprefix=garden%2Bpressure%2Bsprayer%2Clawngarden%2C136&sr=1-8&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=af4d34fb510ec81cf6baa702ba75a7db&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)
* * *

## Plant Symptoms
  * [Bronze or brown patches](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/bronze-spots-or-patches)
  * [Brown or slimy roots](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/roots-are-brown-smelly-or-slimy)
  * [Brown or yellow leaf tips/edges](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/brown-yellow-or-burnt-edges-or-tips)
  * [Buds dying](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/buds-dying)
  * [Buds look odd](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/buds-look-odd)
  * [Bugs are visible](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/i-see-bugs)
  * [Curling or clawing leaves](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/curling-or-clawing-leaves)
  * [Dark leaves](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/dark-green)
  * [Drooping plant](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/drooping-plant)
  * [Holes in leaves](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/holes-in-leaves)
  * [Mold or powder](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/mold-or-white-powder)
  * [Pink or purple on leaves](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/pink-or-purple-on-leaves)
  * [Red stems](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/red-stems)
  * [Shiny or smooth leaves](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/shiny-or-smooth-leaves)
  * [Spots or markings](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/spots-or-markings-on-leaves)
  * [Twisted growth](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/twisted-growth)
  * [Webbing](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/webbing)
  * [Wilting leaves](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/wilting-leaves)
  * [Yellow between leaf veins](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/yellow-between-leaf-veins)
  * [Yellow leaves](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/yellow-leaves)

This page is part of our Plant Doctor series. You can use our tool to filter by symptom and help [diagnose your plant](https://www.growweedeasy.com/cannabis-symptoms-pictures).

[](https://www.instagram.com/growweedeasy/) [](https://www.facebook.com/growweedeasy/) [](https://www.pinterest.com/growweedeasy/) [](https://www.linkedin.com/in/nebula-haze/)
GrowWeedEasy.com was [founded in 2010](https://www.growweedeasy.com/about) by Nebula Haze and Sirius Fourside. Featured in _High Times_ , Ed Rosenthal’s books, _Cannabis Business Times_ , and _Skunk Magazine_ , GrowWeedEasy.com is the **#1 free resource in the world** for learning how to grow cannabis at home. Our simple step-by-step tutorials make it easy for anyone to grow great weed. **[Sign up for our free newsletter](https://www.growweedeasy.com/message/news)** to get grow tips delivered every Sunday at 4:20 AM.
